title: 10 best herbal creams for knee swelling

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal ginger creams contain bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce swelling and pain in the knees.

Analgesic and antirheumatic properties: The active ingredients in herbal ginger creams, such as gingerols and paradol, can help alleviate knee pain by reducing inflammation and suppressing pain transmission, making it an effective natural remedy for knee arthritis and related conditions.

Antioxidant activity: Ginger's antioxidant properties can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation, which can exacerbate knee swelling and joint damage, thereby promoting overall joint health.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with knee swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to reduce swelling and pain in the knees by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. This natural anti-inflammatory effect helps to relax the muscles and improve blood circulation, which in turn reduces knee swelling and discomfort, making it a popular natural remedy for people suffering from knee pain and inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Gingerol: Gingerol has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce knee swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines.
Shogaol: Shogaol is a bioactive compound that has potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, helping to reduce pain and swelling associated with knee inflammation.

Curcuminoid: Curcuminoids, particularly curcumin, have strong anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate knee swelling by reducing oxidative stress and inhibiting the activation of inflammatory pathways.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The underground stem of the plant is used due to its high concentration of active compounds, including gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: The roots of Ginger are used for their ability to reduce pain and inflammation, making them a popular ingredient in creams for knee swelling.
Buds: The buds of Ginger contain high amounts of bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and relieve pain, making them a valuable addition to knee swelling creams.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 10 grams of dried zingiber officinale root in 100ml of carrier oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids after 2 weeks.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.
Add the infused oil to the melted beeswax and shea butter mixture slowly.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: Turmeric creams rich in curcuminoids, particularly curcumin, have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which help reduce knee swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Gingerols and Shogaols: The active compounds gingerols and shogaols in turmeric creams have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate pain and swelling in the knees by reducing prostaglandins, a hormone that causes inflammation.

Corticosteroids: Turmeric creams may also contain natural corticosteroid-like compounds that help reduce knee swelling by suppressing the production of inflammatory cytokines and decreasing tissue damage.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with knee swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, particularly curcumin. This natural compound reduces the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby alleviating swelling and pain in the knees. The curcumin in these creams also has antioxidant properties, which help to neutralize free radicals and promote healing in the affected area. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Curcuma longa creams can provide relief from knee swelling and promote overall joint health.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: This polyphenolic compound has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the knees by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Tumerone: A sesquiterpene found in turmeric oil, tumerone has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate knee swelling and pain by reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.

Demethoxycurcumin: A curcuminoid with potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, demethoxycurcumin can help reduce knee swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and reducing oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They contain a high concentration of essential oils, which are often used in topical creams for their anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: They are a rich source of curcuminoids, which are commonly used in creams to reduce inflammation and alleviate knee swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh curcuma longa rhizomes and clean them thoroughly with cold running water for 5 minutes.
Grind the rhizomes into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine 30g of the powder with 100ml of distilled water in a double boiler and heat it gently for 20 minutes.
Mix the heated mixture with 20g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil in a blender until well combined for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and store it in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours before use.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Bell pepper extract has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory compounds like carotenoids and flavonoids, which may help reduce knee swelling by minimizing inflammation.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in bell peppers can neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress and tissue damage, potentially reducing the severity of knee swelling and improving overall joint health.

Pain-relieving properties: Bell pepper extract contains capsaicin, which has been used to relieve pain and reduce inflammation in various conditions, including osteoarthritis, a common cause of knee swelling.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum, also known as bell pepper, creams helps with knee swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The capsaicin content in these creams penetrates deep into the skin, blocking the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain. This reduction in pain and inflammation helps to alleviate swelling and discomfort associated with knee injuries or conditions like arthritis. Regular application of Capsicum annuum creams can provide relief and promote a smoother recovery process.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicin: Capsaicin helps with knee swelling by reducing inflammation and pain through its ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory substances and blocking the action of pain-sensing neurons.

Quercetin: Quercetin helps with knee swelling by acting as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and preventing the activation of inflammatory cells.

Ellagic acid: Ellagic acid helps with knee swelling by displaying anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, reducing inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and preventing the activation of inflammatory cells.
remedy_parts: Fruits: The most commonly used parts of Capsicum annuum in creams for knee swelling are the fruits, which contain capsaicin and other compounds that help reduce pain and inflammation.
Seeds: The seeds of Capsicum annuum are also used in creams for knee swelling, as they contain capsaicin and other bioactive compounds that help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation.
Leaves: Some creams for knee swelling may use the leaves of Capsicum annuum, which contain capsaicin and other compounds that help reduce pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of ripe capsicum annuum fruits and wash them thoroughly under running water.
Dry the capsicum annuum fruits in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 8 hours.
Grind the dried capsicum annuum fruits into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 100 grams of the capsicum annuum powder with 200 grams of coconut oil and 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture in the double boiler for 20 minutes at 70 degrees celsius to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Herbal mountain arnica creams contain compounds like helenin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, making it effective in reducing knee swelling.
Pain-relieving property: Arnica's methyl salicylate content helps to numb the affected area, providing fast-acting relief from pain and discomfort associated with knee swelling.
Anti-swelling property: The creams' ability to improve circulation and reduce blood stagnation helps to reduce fluid accumulation in the knees, thereby minimizing swelling.

Flavonoids: Specifically, flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin, present in Arnica montana, possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate knee swelling and promote tissue repair.

remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part of Arnica montana, as they contain the highest concentration of arnica oil, which is responsible for its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Leaves: The leaves contain smaller amounts of arnica oil, but are still used in some creams, as they have a more subtle effect and are often used in combination with the flowers.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes (underground stems) contain a smaller amount of arnica oil compared to the flowers, but are used in some creams for their additional anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried arnica montana flowers in the early morning to ensure optimal potency.
Steep 20g of the dried flowers in 200ml of oil like sweet almond oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Mix 10g of beeswax with 20g of shea butter and 10g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Combine the infused oil with the melted wax mixture and stir until smooth consistency is achieved.

remedy_properties: Salicin: Salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, helps reduce inflammation and pain associated with knee swelling by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which cause pain and swelling.

Stems: The stems of Salix alba are used due to their high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce knee swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of salix alba bark in late summer or early fall for its medicinal properties.
Dry the bark in a warm place for 2-3 weeks or until it reaches a crumbly texture.
Steep 2 tablespoons of dried salix alba bark in 1 cup of boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid and add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of coconut oil to it.
Heat the mixture gently for 5-10 minutes or until the beeswax and coconut oil melt and blend.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stinging nettle cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like polysaccharides, flavonoids, and terpenoids that help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the affected area.

Dry the harvested plant material at 40 degrees celsius for 2 hours to remove excess moisture.
Combine 20 grams of dried plant material with 100 grams of carrier oil such as coconut oil in a double boiler.
Steep the mixture in the double boiler for 2 hours to allow the plant's active compounds to infuse into the oil.

Barks: They are used because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in the knees.
remedy_recipe: Weigh and combine 10 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves with 20 grams of base oil in a clean glass container.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60 degrees celsius for 2 hours to infuse the oil.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a separate glass container to remove the solids.
Add 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil and 1 teaspoon of beeswax to the strained oil and heat until melted.
Pour the melted mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and harden before use.

remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana officinalis roots in the morning after the dew has dried to ensure quality.

Infuse 2g of dried roots in 20ml of carrier oil at 60°c for 6 hours to create a tincture.
Mix 5g of beeswax with 10g of shea butter to create a base for the cream.
Combine 10ml of tincture with 50g of base mixture and 20ml of distilled water to create the final cream.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems from a clean and well-drained area in the morning.
Combine the harvested gaultheria procumbens with 1/2 cup of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat.
Steep the gaultheria procumbens mixture for 10-15 minutes then strain it using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Mix 1 tablespoon of beeswax with 2 tablespoons of shea butter and heat the mixture in a double boiler for 5 minutes.
Blend the gaultheria procumbens infused oil with the beeswax and shea butter mixture and pour it into containers for cooling.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with knee swelling because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality lavandula angustifolia flowers and dry them thoroughly within 24 hours.
Grind dried lavender flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine ground lavender with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Remove from heat, let cool and thicken for 15-20 minutes before transferring to sterilized containers.

remedy_recipe: Gather dried boswellia serrata powder, coconut oil, beeswax, vitamin e oil, and distilled water.
Mix 20 grams of boswellia serrata powder with 50 grams of coconut oil in a heat-resistant glass bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the mixture reaches 160°f.
Remove the mixture from heat and stir in 5 grams of beeswax and 2 grams of vitamin e oil until fully incorporated.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes before transferring it to an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cayenne creams helps with knee swelling because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation in the joints. remedy_recipe: Harvest dried capsicum frutescens pods grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 20 grams of the ground capsicum frutescens powder with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at low temperature for 30 minutes stirring occasionally to infuse the oil.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for 2 hours before transferring it to an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey creams helps with knee swelling because of its unique combination of anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh symphytum officinale leaves in the morning for optimal potency and clean them thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the symphytum officinale leaves using a dehydrator on low heat or by air-drying them for 24 hours.
Combine dried symphytum officinale leaves with coconut oil in a 1:10 ratio and steep the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids, then add beeswax and vitamin e oil in a 5:3 ratio.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat until the beeswax is fully melted and the cream has cooled to room temperature before transferring it to sterilized containers for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with knee swelling because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically to the affected area, it helps reduce inflammation and alleviate pain caused by joint conditions such as osteoarthritis. The cream also increases blood flow to the area, which can aid in reducing stiffness and swelling. Additionally, licorice creams contain antioxidants that help protect the joint from further damage, promoting a faster recovery. As a result, herbal licorice creams provide natural relief for knee swelling and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as glycyrrhizin and glabridin, help reduce inflammation in the knee joint, which can contribute to swelling.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in licorice cream, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, protect the knee joint from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate swelling.

Anti-pain and anti-edema properties: Licorice extract contains compounds like isoliquiritigenin and licoricidin that have been shown to reduce pain and inflammation in joints, as well as alleviate edema (swelling) by improving lymphatic drainage.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the joints.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing joint swelling and improving mobility.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root and grind it into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the powdered root with 100 milliliters of distilled water in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes.
Add 50 grams of unsalted butter and 20 grams of beeswax to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated for 5 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to room temperature, allowing it to thicken for 30 minutes.
Transfer the mixture to a sterilized glass jar and store it in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours before applying topically to affected knees.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Devil's claw creams helps with knee swelling because of its unique combination of natural anti-inflammatory compounds, such as harpagosides and iridoid glycosides. These active ingredients work together to reduce inflammation and alleviate joint pain, which can contribute to knee swelling. By applying the cream topically, the active ingredients are able to penetrate deep into the skin and reach the affected joints, providing targeted relief from swelling and discomfort. As a result, herbal devil's claw creams offer a natural and effective way to manage knee swelling and promote overall joint health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Devil's claw contains anti-inflammatory compounds like harpagosides, which help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the knees by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Analgesic properties: The cream's analgesic properties can help numb the affected area, providing relief from knee pain and swelling associated with conditions such as osteoarthritis or rheumatoid arthritis.

Antioxidant activity: Devil's claw's antioxidant properties help protect the knees from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to joint inflammation and degeneration.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, such as harpagosides and glycosides, which help reduce knee swelling.
Rhyzomes: Contain a variety of bioactive compounds, including alkaloids and steroids, that contribute to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Collect and dry harpagophytum procumbens root powder using shade drying for 7-10 days or air circulation.
Mix the dried root powder with beeswax 20%, coconut oil 40%, shea butter 15%, and vitamin e oil 5%.
Melt the beeswax in a double boiler at 160°f for 30 minutes, then add the harpagophytum procumbens powder mixture stirring continuously.
Allow the mixture to cool down to 120°f and blend with a hand blender until smooth and creamy texture forms within 10-15 minutes.
Pour the cream into sterilized glass jars and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months or at room temperature for up to 3 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover creams helps with knee swelling because they contain isoflavones, a type of plant-based estrogen that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically to the affected area, these isoflavones can help reduce inflammation and relieve pain in the knees. Additionally, red clover creams may also promote blood flow to the area, further reducing swelling and promoting healing. The natural ingredients in red clover creams make them a popular alternative treatment for knee swelling and arthritis-related discomfort.
remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Isoflavones, particularly formononetin and biochanin A, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce knee swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.

Saponins: Saponins in red clover cream have been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate pain and reduce swelling associated with knee conditions such as osteoarthritis.
Vitamins and minerals: Red clover cream is rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like calcium and potassium, which are essential for maintaining healthy joints and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the red clover plant are rich in isoflavones, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in knees.
Flowers: Red clover flowers contain triterpenoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making them effective in reducing knee inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 100g of fresh red clover flowers trifolium pratense in a warm oven.
Combine the dried flower material with 20ml of sweet almond oil and 10g of beeswax in a heat-proof container.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 70°c for 30 minutes stirring occasionally to melt the wax.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass jar discarding solids.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for 24 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Balm-of-gilead creams helps with knee swelling because they contain a unique blend of herbal extracts that work together to reduce inflammation and promote healing. The balm's anti-inflammatory properties help to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with swollen knees, while its natural antiseptic qualities prevent infection and promote tissue repair. Additionally, the cream's soothing and warming effects can help to relax tense muscles and improve circulation, further reducing swelling and promoting overall knee health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's ingredients, such as Willow Bark Extract, reduce inflammation and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby relieving knee pain and stiffness.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in the cream, including Vitamin E and Ferulic Acid, help neutralize free radicals that can damage joint tissue and accelerate degenerative processes, reducing the risk of chronic inflammation and swelling.

Pain-relieving properties: The presence of natural pain relievers like Salicylic Acid (a precursor to Aspirin) and Capsaicin helps to reduce knee pain by blocking the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective at reducing knee swelling and alleviating pain.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and improve joint mobility.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried bark of populus balsamifera and grind it into fine powder using mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine ground populus balsamifera powder with 20% olive oil and 10% beeswax in a double boiler and heat for 30 minutes.
Add 5 drops of peppermint essential oil and 5 drops of arnica essential oil to the mixture and stir well for 2 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterile glass containers and let it cool at room temperature for 1 hour before refrigerating for 24 hours.
Remove the mixture from the refrigerator and whip it with a hand mixer for 5 minutes to create a smooth and creamy consistency.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for knee swelling are topical treatments infused with natural ingredients like arnica, aloe vera, and turmeric that help reduce inflammation and alleviate swelling in the knees. These creams work by increasing blood flow, reducing pain, and promoting healing. Examples of herbal creams that help with knee swelling include creams containing arnica montana and capsaicin. They can improve lives by allowing individuals to move freely and engage in daily activities without discomfort, enhancing overall quality of life and well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to knee swelling that are treated with herbal creams are various musculoskeletal conditions, including arthritis, bursitis, and tendonitis. These herbal creams often contain anti-inflammatory ingredients such as arnica, capsaicin, and ginger, which help to reduce pain, inflammation, and stiffness in affected joints. They can also promote healing and relaxation, providing relief from discomfort and improving overall joint health.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with knee swelling is often a blend of arnica, ginger, and comfrey. Arnica cream helps reduce inflammation and soothe pain, while ginger cream promotes blood circulation and warmth to ease stiffness. Comfrey cream supports tissue repair and regeneration, promoting a faster recovery. Applying these creams together in a rotation or alternating between them can provide long-lasting relief from knee swelling, promoting healthy healing and flexibility. Consult a healthcare professional for personalized advice.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Phytomedicine : international journal of phytotherapy and phytopharmacology, herbal creams can be beneficial for knee swelling due to their anti-inflammatory properties and ability to enhance collagen production. In the study, rats with surgically transected medial collateral ligaments (MCLs) were treated with a herbal remedy or plain adhesive plaster, and the results showed that the herbal remedy significantly improved the ultimate tensile strength (UTS) and stiffness of repairing MCLs at 3 and 6 weeks after injury. This suggests that herbal creams may promote healing and reduce swelling in knee injuries by enhancing tissue strength and elasticity.
